stories.title: Sinner Survives Five-Set Scare to Open Wimbledon Title Defense

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Jannik Sinner survives five sets in his Wimbledon opener.

4-6, 6-3, 6-7(6), 6-2, 6-3 over Kecmanovic. Three and a half hours. The defending champion nearly stumbled — then didn't.

Next up: Borges in Round 2. 🎾 #Wimbledon2026

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Sinner skipped every grass warm-up this year.

Then nearly lost his Wimbledon opener in five sets vs Kecmanovic (4-6, 6-3, 6-7, 6-2, 6-3).

Still defending. Still dangerous. But not without a scare. #Wimbledon2026

article.headline: Defending Champion Sinner Survives Kecmanovic in Five Sets to Open Wimbledon Title Defense

stories.title: Djokovic Wins Opening Match — Then Asks for a Wedding Invitation

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Novak Djokovic saw a fan propose to his partner on Centre Court during his match Monday.

His response: "I want an invitation to the wedding."

He also won 6-4, 5-7, 6-4, 6-4. Now 21-0 in Wimbledon first rounds. 🎾 #Wimbledon2026

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Novak Djokovic: 21-0 in Wimbledon first rounds.

He beat Wu Yibing in four sets Monday. Next: Tsitsipas in Round 2.

Seven titles. Still going. #Wimbledon2026 #ATPTour

article.headline: Djokovic Beats Wu Yibing in Four Sets, Goes Viral with Centre Court Marriage Proposal Response

stories.title: Draper Withdraws from Wimbledon — Arm Injury Forces Out British No. 1 Before Fritz Clash

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Jack Draper has withdrawn from Wimbledon.

Arm injury has returned — the same one that cost him the Australian Open and Roland-Garros.

Lucky loser Dusan Lajovic will now face Fritz in Round 1. #Wimbledon2026

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Jack Draper on his Wimbledon withdrawal: "Devastated to share that I have had to withdraw from my first round match due to a recurrence of my arm injury."

Third major in a row missed or withdrawn. #Wimbledon2026

article.headline: Draper Withdraws from Wimbledon with Arm Injury Recurrence — Lucky Loser Lajovic Steps In for Fritz Clash

stories.title: Women's Day 1 — Sabalenka, Gauff, Andreeva Advance; Zheng and Andreescu Fall

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Women's Day 1 at Wimbledon:

• Sabalenka def. Kostovic 6-2, 6-3 (64 min)
• Gauff def. Korpatsch 6-2, 6-1 (54 min — her fastest Wimbledon win)
• Andreeva def. Linette 7-5, 6-4

All three through with authority. #Wimbledon2026

article.headline: Sabalenka and Gauff Cruise; Zheng and Andreescu Fall as Women's Draw Reshapes on Wimbledon Day 1

stories.title: Men's Day 1 Upsets — Ruud and Rublev Crash Out in First Round

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rublev (No. 12) lost to Safiullin at Wimbledon in a five-set match that went to 12-10 in the final tiebreak.

Ruud (No. 11) fell in straight sets to Hurkacz.

Four men's seeds gone on Day 1. The draw is wide open. #Wimbledon2026

article.headline: Ruud and Rublev Exit Wimbledon on Day 1 — Four Seeds Fall as Draw Opens Up
